currentSliceTitle: Implement first neutral dwelling and neutral-unit slice
currentSliceNotes: Completed the first neutral-content implementation slice: authored neutral unit records outside the six faction ladders, added neutral dwelling families with recruit/guard data, converted selected generic neutral dwelling sites to neutral rosters, and wired ContentService, OverworldRules, BattleRules, repo validation, and focused core smoke coverage so neutral recruit sources and neutral encounters can use real neutral rosters. This is a first slice, not a complete neutral ecology; existing scenarios were preserved except for truthful generic site data.

realityAssessment.notes: Existing systems and validation harnesses remain useful foundations, but do not count as product completion until proven through live-client manual play.

phases.title: Honest reset, parity ledger, and removal of fake-complete language
phases.focus: Planning docs now state the prototype/pre-alpha reality and reset the roadmap around manual play evidence.

phases.title: Make River Pass manually completable end-to-end
phases.focus: AcOrP reported River Pass manual completion gate passed on 2026-04-18, covering the live-client overworld, town, battle, save/resume, victory, defeat, and outcome-routing gate.

phases.title: Playable alpha baseline with 2 fully realized factions
phases.focus: Use the faction bible as the six-faction content scaffold source, then deepen selected alpha factions through scenario, town, battle, AI, save/load, and manual-play slices before claiming playability.

phases.title: Production alpha layer for external playtest readiness
phases.focus: Coherent placeholder art/audio, settings, export/build pipeline, logs, onboarding, and external playtest workflow.

phases.title: HoMM2-class breadth
phases.focus: Playable broad content package with multiple factions, maps, campaigns, AI, and reliable save/load.

phases.title: HoMM3-class breadth
phases.focus: Later strategic depth expansion after HoMM2-class breadth is stable and playable.

activeMilestone.goal: A real player can complete River Pass from the shipped live client without editor-only steps or developer-only knowledge.
acceptanceCriteria: River Pass launches from the live menu path chosen for the milestone.
The opening UI communicates objective, threat, and a useful first action.
The overworld map is readable enough for movement, route choice, site interaction, town entry, and encounter entry.
At least one owned town supports useful recruitment or recovery decisions that affect completion.
At least one battle is entered through normal overworld play and can be resolved through visible tactical controls.
Battle state clearly exposes active side, legal actions, target selection, and result.
Post-battle survivors and losses sync back to overworld state.
One victory path is reachable by normal play.
One defeat path is reachable and routes coherently.
Save/resume works from overworld, town, and battle states used by the scenario.
Victory and defeat outcomes route through the normal outcome and menu flow.
The scenario is manually completed at least twice from a clean profile with notes captured.

steps.title: Reset project and plan docs to the prototype/pre-alpha reality
Draft a six-faction content bible with no commons or shared faction skeletons
Start real six-faction implementation with JSON content scaffolds and validation
Define overworld biomes, objects, buildings, and resource grammar
Implement biome and richer overworld object content domains
Implement first real neutral dwellings and neutral units for overworld and battle
Run a live-client manual audit of River Pass and record the first real player blockers
Create the River Pass parity ledger for overworld, town, battle, save/resume, victory, and defeat
Fix launch, objective, opening guidance, and overworld readability blockers
Recover owned-town usability for recruitment, recovery, garrison, and return routing
Recover battle usability for turn clarity, legal actions, targeting, enemy turns, and battle resolution
Tune River Pass so one fair victory path and one coherent defeat path are reachable
Prove save/resume from overworld, town, and battle during the real scenario path
Prove victory and defeat outcome routing plus restart and menu return behavior
Complete River Pass manually at least twice from a clean profile and capture remaining friction

nextActions: Add one narrow manually-audited guarded-neutral-dwelling placement only after proving its overworld entry, battle resolution, rewards, and save/resume behavior in the live client.
Expand neutral ecology with wandering stacks, neutral banks, surrender/recruit diplomacy, and biome-specific neutral palettes after this first neutral dwelling foundation stays stable.
Place selected new overworld object families in one narrow scenario slice only after defining truthful encounter, transit, and service behavior for that scenario.
Implement paired transit traversal or guarded-bank encounter resolution before placing transit objects or guarded reward sites as player-facing route-critical content.
Integrate selected scaffold factions into scenario placements, army groups, encounters, overworld landmarks, AI fronts, and manual play notes.
Capture a short parity-ledger note for River Pass recording AcOrP's passed manual gate so the milestone stays documented beyond chat history.
Avoid pretending HoMM3-class overworld breadth exists until richer biomes, objects, and repeated manual-play scenarios actually land.
